selection.rule: Traverse upstream source order; retain items for which Qwen token validation accepts both concept tokens and both behavior tokens, and retain an item only when neither concept has appeared in an earlier retained pair; stop after 20 pairs. No model score or intervention result enters selection.

anchor_suffix: Keep the answer private and hold it in mind.

forbidden_in_each_pair_cue: the paired foil's canonical concept surface
the pair's target behavior answer surface
the pair's foil behavior answer surface
audit_policy.behavior_prompt_overlap: Reject a cue with a contiguous run of 6 or more normalized word tokens shared with any upstream behavior prompt.
audit_policy.split_hygiene: cue_id, fact_id, and normalized cue text must be globally unique; train and heldout fact_id sets must be disjoint; heldout cues must use independent facts rather than paraphrases of train cues.
audit_policy.rendering: Every concept uses each train template exactly once and each heldout template exactly once. Record the final token of the identical anchor suffix; reject truncation or a non-identical anchor tokenization.
audit_policy.direction_sign: Orient only as normalized(train concept mean minus matched leave-pair-out baseline mean). Never flip using heldout labels or J-Lens cosine.
audit_policy.baseline: For each train template slot, average all other concepts except the concept and its paired foil; subtract that matched-template mean before averaging slots and normalizing.

validation_plan.no_layer_selection_rule: Layer 18 and the full preregistered band are fixed before heldout scores are inspected.
validation_plan.sign_check: At layer 18, at least 80 percent of concepts must have positive heldout concept-minus-leave-pair-out score, and the concept-level bootstrap 95 percent confidence interval for the macro mean must exclude zero. A failed concept is reported and its direction is not flipped.
validation_plan.heldout_classification: Using train-only centering and scaling for each direction, report per-prompt 40-way top-1 accuracy, mean reciprocal rank, and macro one-vs-rest AUROC over all 80 heldout cues. Require the top-1 bootstrap 95 percent lower bound above 1/40 chance, AUROC lower bound above 0.5, and a fixed-label permutation p-value below 0.01.
validation_plan.known_answer_control: Run separate explicit-answer versions of the 80 heldout cues; their activations never enter direction fitting or validation. Report exact target-token top-1 and top-5 rates; require top-1 at least 0.50 and top-5 at least 0.80.
validation_plan.silent_output_contamination: On the 240 silent prompts, report target-token ranks. Require aggregate target top-1 rate at most 0.10 and top-10 rate at most 0.25; otherwise label the MD estimator as contaminated by output planning.
validation_plan.stability: From cached train activations, recompute each direction leaving out one cue slot at a time. Require median cosine to the full direction of at least 0.70 at layer 18 and report every concept-layer value.
validation_plan.jlens_alignment: Report cosine(MD, raw W_U J) and cosine(MD, RMS-gain-folded W_U J) per concept and layer with concept-bootstrap intervals. Alignment is diagnostic only and never changes sign, selection, or pass status.

cues.text: A sea reptile returns to a sandy beach to bury a clutch of eggs.
A slow pond dweller basks on a log and dives when a hiker approaches.
A long-lived chelonian with a beaked mouth migrates between feeding and nesting grounds.
A hatchling follows the brightest horizon from its nest toward the ocean.
A painted freshwater reptile suns itself on rocks beside a quiet pond.
A leatherback is the largest living member of this reptile group and feeds mainly on jellyfish.
A limbless reptile samples airborne chemicals with a forked tongue.
A python coils around a branch before swallowing prey whole.
A cobra spreads a hood when threatened.
A desert reptile warns hikers by vibrating a rattle at the end of its tail.
A boa subdues prey by wrapping muscular coils around it.
This elongated reptile periodically sheds its old skin in one piece.
A shaggy, hump-shouldered bovine grazes near geothermal valleys in Yellowstone.
The old American nickel once depicted this heavy plains mammal.
A wallow in dry grass marks where a horned herd animal rolled in dust.
A wild bison herd crosses open prairie during a seasonal migration.
A famous Wild West showman took this animal as his nickname.
This plains mammal lends its name to a large city on Lake Erie.
An arachnid produces silk from spinnerets to protect an egg sac.
A tarantula waits at the mouth of a burrow for passing prey.
Pedipalps and book lungs are characteristic anatomy of this small predator.
A black widow carries an hourglass-shaped marking beneath its abdomen.
A small arachnid controls crop pests by ambushing insects among leaves.
A jumping species stalks prey using large forward-facing eyes.
Players dribble an orange ball and shoot it through a raised hoop.
A free throw is taken from a line facing a backboard and rim.
A point guard leads a fast break down a hardwood court.
A slam dunk sends the ball directly through the basket.
A shot from beyond the arc earns more points than a closer attempt.
A center battles for a rebound after a missed jump shot.
A pitcher throws from a mound toward a batter at home plate.
A home run can clear the outfield fence.
Teams alternate batting and fielding on a diamond.
A catcher wears protective gear behind the plate.
The sport is tracked with innings, strikes, and earned-run averages.
A shortstop fields a grounder and throws toward first base.
A sommelier evaluates a vintage poured from a corked bottle.
A cellar stores bottles so they can age before dinner service.
Tannins and terroir are common terms when discussing this fermented drink.
A stemmed glass is swirled to release the drink's aroma.
A sparkling form of this alcoholic drink is often opened for celebrations.
A dry or sweet alcoholic drink is paired with cheese by a restaurant expert.
A foamy drink is drawn from a keg through a tap.
A brewery combines water, yeast, and hops to make this beverage.
Lager, stout, and pilsner are varieties of this drink.
A pint served in a pub develops a frothy head.
An India pale ale is a hoppy style of this beverage.
A brewer monitors fermentation before filling cans and bottles.
A bird of prey glides almost silently because of specialized wing feathers.
A round facial disc channels faint sounds toward this raptor's ears.
A barn-dwelling hunter coughs up pellets containing tiny bones.
A wide-eyed raptor grips prey with sharp talons.
This bird's asymmetric ears help it locate rodents beneath snow.
A small burrowing species of this raptor nests underground in grasslands.
A large raptor builds an eyrie high on a cliff.
A hooked beak and exceptional long-distance vision help this bird hunt.
A fish-eating raptor snatches prey from a lake with powerful talons.
This soaring bird rides mountain thermals with broad wings spread.
A large mountain species of this raptor nests across northern ranges.
Roman legions carried a standard topped by this bird.

A northern-hemisphere month known for hawthorn and lily-of-the-valley birth flowers.
The calendar month whose name is also a modal verb expressing possibility.

The month immediately after April.
The month when the United States celebrates Independence Day.
The midsummer month named for Julius Caesar.
The first full month of astronomical summer in the Northern Hemisphere.
A school-holiday month often associated with fireworks in the United States.
Bastille Day occurs during this month.
The Wimbledon tennis finals are usually held during this month.
The atmospheric gas used to create an inert blanket in food packaging.
The element whose diatomic molecules have an exceptionally strong triple bond.

The nutrient element plants often obtain through fixation by soil bacteria.
The relatively unreactive gas used to pressurize some aircraft tires.

The gas consumed by cells during aerobic respiration.

A reactive gas that supports combustion.
